title: A finite row application is additive in the input

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: A finite row applied to the pointwise sum of two inputs equals the sum of the two finite row applications.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
a, x, and y are real-valued functions on s
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite row application distributes over pointwise addition.

declarations.name: finiteRowApply
declarations.statement: def finiteRowApply {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, a i * x i

declarations.name: finiteRowApply_add
declarations.statement: theorem finiteRowApply_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x y : ι → Real) : finiteRowApply s a (fun i => x i + y i) = finiteRowApply s a x + finiteRowApply s a y

tasks.input: Translate additivity of a finite row application into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteRowApply_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x y : ι → Real) : finiteRowApply s a (fun i => x i + y i) = finiteRowApply s a x + finiteRowApply s a y

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding finiteRowApply, using finite-sum additivity, and proving the pointwise algebraic equality.
Find the finite-sum additivity lemma needed for this proof.
